metrics.name: Debt to Equity
metrics.description: Total debt divided by total equity

metrics.name: Debt to Assets
metrics.description: Total debt divided by total assets

metrics.name: Equity Multiplier
metrics.description: Total assets divided by total equity

metrics.name: Debt to EBITDA
metrics.description: Total debt divided by EBITDA. Common credit metric used by lenders and rating agencies. NOTE: Ensure EBITDA includes all adjustments for one-time items, stock compensation, and other addbacks per credit agreement definitions. total_debt should match the definition in loan covenants (typically includes all interest-bearing debt, may exclude certain items). Values < 3.0x are generally considered conservative for most industries.

metrics.name: Interest Coverage
metrics.description: EBITDA divided by interest expense. Measures ability to service debt from operating earnings. NOTE: This metric uses EBITDA and is sensitive to depreciation and amortization capture. Interest capitalization may affect comparability across periods or entities. Values > 1.5x are generally considered healthy for investment-grade credits.

metrics.name: Debt Service Coverage
metrics.description: EBITDA divided by total debt service (interest + principal). Measures ability to service both interest and principal from operating cash flow. NOTE: This metric assumes standard EBITDA construction. Principal payments are not tax-deductible, so this ratio may understate true coverage. For PIK interest, ensure interest_expense includes both cash and PIK components. Values > 1.25x are typically required by lenders.
